name: Ryan v. Schad
question: Did the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit abuse its discretion by failing to issue the mandate for execution after the U.S. Supreme Court declined to issue a writ of certiorari?
description: A case in which the Court determined that, once the Court has denied a petition for a writ of certiorari to an inmate on death row, the appellate court must issue a mandate for execution immediately absent extraordinary circumstances.
